response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,5]],"date-time":"2026-05-05T12:21:11Z","timestamp":1777983671590,"version":"3.51.4"},"reference-count":52,"publisher":"Springer Science and Business Media LLC","issue":"1","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,13]],"date-time":"2026-03-13T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1773360000000},"content-version":"tdm","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"https:\/\/creativecommons.org\/licenses\/by\/4.0"},{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,17]],"date-time":"2026-03-17T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1773705600000},"content-version":"vor","delay-in-days":4,"URL":"https:\/\/creativecommons.org\/licenses\/by\/4.0"}],"content-domain":{"domain":["link.springer.com"],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":["Nat Commun"],"abstract":"<jats:title>Abstract<\/jats:title>\n                  <jats:p>Nanofluidic scattering microscopy characterizes single molecules in subwavelength nanofluidic channels label-free, using the interference of visible light scattered by the molecule and nanochannel. It determines a molecule\u2019s hydrodynamic radius by tracking its diffusion trajectory and its molecular weight by analyzing its scattering intensity along that trajectory. However, using standard analysis algorithms, it is limited to characterization of proteins larger than \u00a0\u2248 60 kDa. Here, we push this limit by one order of magnitude to below \u00a0\u2248 6 kDa molecular weight and \u00a0\u2248 1.5 nm hydrodynamic radius \u2014 as we exemplify on the peptide hormone insulin \u2014 by using ultrasmall nanofluidic channels and by analyzing the data with a hierarchical vision transformer. When we benchmark this approach against the theoretical limit set by the Cram\u00e9r\u2013Rao Lower Bound, we find that it can be approached with sufficiently long molecular trajectories. This enables quantitative label-free single-molecule microscopy for biologically relevant families of sub-10-kDa molecules, such as cytokines, chemokines and peptide hormones.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1038\/s41467-026-70514-z","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,13]],"date-time":"2026-03-13T01:25:12Z","timestamp":1773365112000},"update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.1007\/springer_crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Label-free mass and size characterization of few-kDa biomolecules by hierarchical vision transformer augmented nanofluidic scattering microscopy"],"prefix":"10.1038","volume":"17","author":[{"given":"Henrik","family":"K.
